methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Extropic is a Series A chip design company operating within the Semiconductors sector, where the private comparables universe includes 8 peers and public anchors span 3 major semiconductor names (NVDA, AMD, ALAB). The target has raised $50M+ to date.

Two valuation methods triangulate defensible range. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $50M raised proxy, implying $100M. Peer-median benchmarking across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—Substrate ($1.0B Series A), Rain AI ($500M Series A), Positron AI ($1.0B Series B), and Etched ($5.0B Series B)—yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 20.3x across NVDA, AMD, ALAB) is not applicable without disclosed revenue.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $85M to $1.1B, with a median of $550M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation discipline: Stage-progression implies floor valuations around $100M, while the peer median—driven by two later-stage Series B comps—pulls substantially higher. The key sensitivity is positioning relative to the Substrate and Rain AI Series A cohort versus the elevated Series B comps. Absent revenue visibility typical of early-stage hardware, comparables-based judgment dominates over fundamental approaches.
